sanskrit: यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत् । यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ॥
hindiTranslation: यज्ञ, दान और तपरूप कर्मोंका त्याग नहीं करना चाहिये, प्रत्युत उनको तो करना ही चाहिये क्योंकि यज्ञ, दान और तप  ये तीनों ही कर्म मनीषियोंको पवित्र करनेवाले हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Sacrifice, giving, and disciplined effort should not be abandoned; they must be done. These three actions purify thoughtful people.
essence: What purifies you should be done, not dropped.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Sacrifice, giving, and disciplined effort should not be dropped. They are actions that purify a thoughtful person.
simpleInsight: Doing what cleanses you matters more than escaping effort.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still trapped between renouncing action and acting rightly. Krishna answers by naming three acts that should never be abandoned: sacrifice, giving, and disciplined effort. He is narrowing renunciation into a cleaner, truer form.
modernRelevance: You want to skip the donation, the difficult conversation, the morning practice. Something in you calls it optional. Krishna says these are exactly the acts that clear the mind.
translationLiteral: यज्ञ (sacrifice) / दान (giving) / तपःकर्म (discipline-action) / न (not) / त्याज्यम् (to be abandoned) / कार्यम् (to be done) / एव (indeed) / तत् (that) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / दानम् (giving) / तपः (disciplined effort) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / पावनानि (purifying) / मनीषिणाम् (of thoughtful people)
insight: It shifts renunciation from avoidance to purification through action.
